task_text: Look at the sprint 14 assignments and testing log, then tell me which tasks are at risk of missing the sprint deadline. Write your assessment to sprint14_deadline_risk.txt.

content.text: I’ve reviewed the sprint assignments and QA results and spotted several deadline risks. I’ll draft the assessment now to capture the full risk analysis.

content.content:
Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

content.text: I’ve identified the tasks most likely to miss the sprint deadline and traced the blocking dependencies — now I’ll compile the findings into your risk report.

input.content:
Project Helios - Sprint 14 Deadline Risk Assessment
Sprint Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18
Analysis Date: Based on assignments as of 2026-07-11 testing log
Author: Automated Risk Analysis

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
-----------------
Sprint 14 has 6 of 8 tasks still open with 3 distinct risk flags already set in the assignment file itself (Blocked, At Risk, Not Started). Based on task assignments and testing log evidence, 3 tasks are CRITICAL/HIGH risk of missing the 2026-07-18 deadline, and 3 additional tasks are MEDIUM risk due to dependencies and test failures.
Historical context amplifies risk: Q2 checklist shows UAT provisioning (HEL-246) already OVERDUE since 2026-07-01, Performance Benchmark (HEL-247) OVERDUE since 2026-07-05, and Integration Test Setup OVERDUE since 2026-06-30 and rescheduled to 2026-07-20 (past sprint end).

TASK-BY-TASK RISK ANALYSIS
--------------------------

[CRITICAL RISK] HEL-243 - Data ingestion: date format normalization
Assignee: Amir Hassan | Due: 2026-07-15 | Status: Blocked | Points: 5
Risk Rationale:
 - Officially Blocked in assignments.
 - Testing log defect HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format - FAIL TC-505. Severity HIGH, affects ~40% of Meridian source data.
 - Blocked pending Meridian sample data due 2026-07-15 - same day as task due date, leaving zero buffer. Status update notes 2-3 day delay expected from Meridian IT clarification.
 - Second HIGH severity defect HEL-D-015 also owned by Amir (Large file ingestion >200k records memory overflow - FAIL TC-507). Owner overloaded with two HIGH defects.
 - Risk Register R-01 (High/High, Score 9) explicitly calls out this blocker as active in sprint 14.
 - Blocks downstream HEL-245.
Mitigation is external dependency (Meridian IT) - low control.

[CRITICAL RISK] HEL-246 - UAT environment: server provisioning
Assignee: Nadia Petrov | Due: 2026-07-16 | Status: At Risk | Points: 8
Risk Rationale:
 - Explicitly flagged as "At Risk" in assignments - largest point task tied for 8 points with HEL-242.
 - Already OVERDUE per Q2 Deliverables Checklist: was due 2026-07-01 and still not complete.
 - Risk Register R-04: UAT timeline slippage - High likelihood, 1-week extension already consumed.
 - Risk Register R-06: Meridian IT environment access delays - Active, awaiting IT infrastructure diagram, owner Nadia Petrov - same owner as task.
 - No progress noted in testing log for UAT.
High probability of slipping past both 2026-07-16 due date and 2026-07-18 sprint boundary.

[HIGH RISK] HEL-247 - Performance benchmark: dashboard load
Assignee: Derek Okafor | Due: 2026-07-18 | Status: Not Started | Points: 3
Risk Rationale:
 - Status Not Started as of testing log period (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11), with due date at sprint end.
 - Historically OVERDUE: Q2 checklist shows Performance benchmarking report was due 2026-07-05 (owner Derek Okafor) - now 6+ days overdue before sprint even started.
 - Testing log Dashboard suite: TC-604 Load time benchmark (100k records) = NOT RUN.
 - Risk Register R-03: Dashboard performance on large datasets - Medium/High Score 6, note "benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started".
 - Risk Register R-05: Key person dependency on Derek Okafor - no backup tech lead.
Pattern shows chronic delay for this owner/task type.

[HIGH RISK] HEL-248 - GeoSync license renewal coordination
Assignee: Raj Patel | Due: 2026-07-14 | Status: In Progress | Points: 2
Risk Rationale:
 - Due July 14 - earliest remaining deadline, giving only 3 days from last test log date (July 11).
 - Risk Register R-02: GeoSync license not renewed before demo - Medium/High Score 6, legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14 same as task.
 - Status Update lists as Blocker #2: Third-party mapping library license renewal pending, legal reviewing, target resolution 2026-07-14.
 - External dependency on Legal team - not fully in control of assignee.
 - Low points but blocking for dashboard mapping features (HEL-242).

[MEDIUM RISK] HEL-242 - Dashboard: chart rendering module
Assignee: Tomas Rivera | Due: 2026-07-16 | Status: In Progress | Points: 8 (largest)
Risk Rationale:
 - Largest effort task (8 points) with only 2 days less than sprint end.
 - Testing log Dashboard suite: TC-602 Chart rendering - line chart = IN PROGRESS, TC-603 bar chart = NOT RUN. Out of 4 dashboard tests, only 1 complete (TC-601 data table). Indicates chart module not yet testable.
 - Depends indirectly on HEL-248 (GeoSync license for mapping) and HEL-241.
Moderate risk of partial completion.

[MEDIUM RISK] HEL-245 - Integration test suite: data pipeline
Assignee: Kenji Watanabe | Due: 2026-07-18 | Status: In Progress | Points: 5
Risk Rationale:
 - QA is same owner (Kenji Watanabe is QA engineer per testing log). Testing log shows Data Pipeline suite result 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL due to HEL-D-014 and HEL-D-015 which are owned by Amir, not Kenji.
 - Cannot achieve 100% pass/sign-off until HEL-243 unblocked and HEL-D-015 fixed. Since HEL-243 is Critical Risk and due July 15, this task's completion on July 18 is dependent.
 - Q2 checklist: Integration test suite setup was OVERDUE (due June 30, rescheduled to July 20) - note its new target is AFTER sprint end, suggesting original sprint goal already slipped.

[LOW RISK / ON TRACK] HEL-241 - Dashboard: data table component
Assignee: Fatima Al-Rashid | Due: 2026-07-14 | Status: In Progress | Points: 5
Risk Rationale:
 - Testing log TC-601 Data table rendering (10k records) = PASS. Suggests core functionality working.
 - Due July 14 but appears ahead of chart module. Likely to meet deadline unless blocked by integration issues.

[DONE] HEL-244 - Integration test suite: auth flows
Assignee: Kenji Watanabe | Due: 2026-07-14 | Status: Done
Testing log confirms 4/4 PASS (TC-401 to TC-404). No risk.

DEPENDENCY MAP
--------------
HEL-D-014 / HEL-243 (Blocked) -> HEL-245 (data pipeline tests cannot pass) -> Sprint Goal "Resolve data format inconsistencies" and Milestone Integration Test Sign-off (target 2026-07-25)
HEL-248 (license) -> HEL-242 (chart rendering may need GeoSync) -> Dashboard v1 (target 2026-07-22)
HEL-246 (UAT provisioning) is independent but on critical path for next sprint.

SPRINT DEADLINE RISK SUMMARY
----------------------------
At Risk of Missing Deadline (July 18):
 - CRITICAL: HEL-243, HEL-246 (will likely miss)
 - HIGH: HEL-247, HEL-248 (likely to miss or slip to last day with no buffer)
 - MEDIUM: HEL-242, HEL-245 (at risk due to dependencies)

Expected completion by sprint end: 2 of 8 tasks (HEL-244 Done + HEL-241 likely) = 25-37% if critical blockers not resolved. 5 of 8 tasks carry HIGH or CRITICAL risk flags.

RECOMMENDATIONS
---------------
1. HEL-243: Escalate to Meridian IT immediately; request sample data before July 15, and consider interim workaround normalizing MM/DD/YYYY in code without waiting for full sample. Reassign HEL-D-015 to another dev to reduce Amir load.
2. HEL-246: Escalate R-06, require Meridian IT infrastructure diagram today. Consider partial environment provisioning.
3. HEL-247: Pair Derek with backup (per R-05) or reassign benchmark to someone else; define minimum viable benchmark criteria.
4. HEL-248: Daily check-in with Legal to ensure July 14 deadline met; prepare risk communication that dashboard demo may need GeoSync disabled.
5. Sprint scope renegotiation: Given Q2 overdue items and current fails (10/15 tests executed, 2 FAIL), recommend moving HEL-246 and HEL-247 to Sprint 15 and focusing Sprint 14 close-out on unblocking HEL-243 -> HEL-245.

content.text:
Risk assessment completed and written to **sprint14_deadline_risk.txt**.

**Summary of tasks at risk:**

**Critical Risk (will likely miss):**
- **HEL-243** Data ingestion: date format normalization - *Blocked*, fails TC-505 (HEL-D-014 affects 40% Meridian data), blocked pending Meridian sample due same day as task due date (2026-07-15), plus second HIGH defect HEL-D-015 owned by same dev
- **HEL-246** UAT environment: server provisioning - *At Risk* in assignments, already OVERDUE since 2026-07-01 per Q2 checklist, Risk R-04 & R-06 active, owner waiting on Meridian IT diagram

**High Risk:**
- **HEL-247** Performance benchmark - *Not Started* with due date 2026-07-18, was already OVERDUE since 2026-07-05 per Q2 checklist, TC-604 NOT RUN, key-person dependency risk R-05
- **HEL-248** GeoSync license renewal - Due earliest (2026-07-14), blocked on Legal review per Risk R-02 and status blocker #2

**Medium Risk (dependency):**
- **HEL-242** Chart rendering module (8pts) - Dashboard suite TC-602 IN PROGRESS, TC-603 NOT RUN
- **HEL-245** Data pipeline test suite - Blocked by HEL-243 fixes, 5/7 PASS with 2 FAIL open, rescheduled target now after sprint end (2026-07-20)

**On Track:** HEL-241 (TC-601 PASS), HEL-244 Done (4/4 auth tests PASS).

Full analysis with dependency map and recommendations is in the file.
